title: Review and setlist: Leslie Odom Jr. brings Broadway to the Boston Pops
author: Shannon Murphy
bullets: Leslie Odom Jr. performed with the Boston Pops at Symphony Hall on May 22, celebrating the Pops' 140th season.
The concert featured American classics like 'The Star-Spangled Banner' and Odom Jr.'s renditions of 'Fly Me To The Moon'.
Odom Jr. captivated the audience with songs from 'Hamilton', including 'Wait For It' and 'Alexander Hamilton'.

title: Chelsea man facing slew of assault charges for alleged violence against young children
author: Darin Zullo
bullets: Kener Bautista-Zepeda, 19, faces 12 charges, including assault, endangerment, and suffocation of children, in Chelsea, Massachusetts.
Incidents involved burning a child with a cigarette lighter and using a heated fork on another's arm, prosecutors allege.
Bautista-Zepeda was located in Virginia after fleeing Massachusetts; he's held without bail pending a May 28 hearing.

title: Concord man, 73, convicted of raping 20-year-old in Sandwich restaurant bathroom in 2023
author: Darin Zullo
bullets: Elie Jammal, 73, from Concord, convicted of raping a 20-year-old in a Sandwich restaurant bathroom in 2023.
Jammal received a 13 ½ to 15-year state prison sentence, followed by 5 years probation for additional assault charges.
Jammal assaulted the victim after making suggestive comments, with court citing his 'shocking sense of entitlement.'

title: Newton Church, founded in 1893, is becoming condos
author: Morgan Rousseau
bullets: Newton's Church of the Open Word, built in 1893, will be converted into six condos and a separate building with 26 units.
The church was once affiliated with the Swedenborgian Church of North America and closed in 2020.
Developer Habitech Communities purchased the property for $4 million after previous plans for an arts center fell through.

title: 5 players the Celtics could target this offseason outside of Giannis Antetokounmpo
author: Colin McCarthy
bullets: Boston Celtics may target Kawhi Leonard for his defensive skills and championship experience if Giannis Antetokounmpo is unavailable.
LeBron James, approaching free agency, remains a long-shot option for the Celtics due to his family ties in Los Angeles.
Forwards John Collins and Jaxson Hayes offer potential frontcourt depth, with Hayes providing affordable center reinforcement.

title: 2 local college baseball programs advance to NCAA World Series
author: Kaley Brown
bullets: Bentley University advanced to the NCAA Division II World Series after defeating Molloy University in a three-game Super Regional series.
Endicott College reached its fourth consecutive Division III College World Series by defeating Salve Regina University in the Super Regional.
Bentley and Endicott await their championship opponents and game schedules in the NCAA World Series tournaments.

title: Celtics reportedly plan to work out UConn basketball star ahead of NBA Draft
author: Kaley Brown
bullets: The Boston Celtics plan to work out UConn star Alex Karaban next week, ahead of the 2026 NBA Draft.
Alex Karaban, a Southborough native, was a key player for UConn, winning two NCAA championships and earning All-Big East First-team honors.
Karaban finished his college career with 1,880 points, ranking sixth all-time for UConn, and leading in games played and three-pointers made.

title: Review & setlist: Charlie Puth turns MGM Music Hall into a masterclass in musicianship
author: Samantha Genzer
bullets: Charlie Puth showcased jazz, pop, and nostalgia at MGM Music Hall in Fenway on May 22, 2026.
Puth's 'Whatever's Clever! World Tour' combines new album songs with older hits, emphasizing his musicianship and vocal talent.
Puth, a Berklee alumnus, reflected on Boston ties and performed with a jazz-club aesthetic, engaging fans as 'Professor Puth.'

title: Review and setlist: Jakob Dylan’s choice to channel Tom Petty pays off at Beverly Wallflowers show
author: Peter Chianca
bullets: The Wallflowers played their entire 1996 album 'Bringing Down the Horse' at The Cabot in Beverly on May 22, 2026.
After performing their album, the band covered Tom Petty's 'Long After Dark' in its entirety, showcasing lesser-known Petty tracks.
Jakob Dylan's vocal style has evolved, with a more brusque delivery providing new dynamics to both Wallflowers and Petty songs.

title: Here’s where people are moving — and leaving — in Mass., according to new U.S. Census data
author: Morgan Rousseau
bullets: Boston saw a decrease of 1,338 residents, leading the state in population decline last year.
Everett and Worcester recorded the highest population gains, adding 2,362 and 1,916 residents respectively.
Massachusetts' population grew by 0.2% last year, with a total increase of 15,500 residents.
